nuggets.headline: Billie Eilish’s parents gave up their bedroom for her music studio
nuggets.text: Before becoming a global phenomenon, Billie Eilish Pirate Baird O’Connell was a home-schooled kid from Los Angeles who started writing music at 11. Her actor parents, Maggie Baird and Patrick O'Connell, were so supportive of her and her brother Finneas's musical passions that they gave up their own bedroom so Billie could have her own room and Finneas's old bedroom could permanently be their home recording studio. There's no soundproofing, but it's where all her biggest hits, including this one, were made.

source.title: Billie Eilish: At 17 the multiple Grammy-nominee says she is coming out of the "joyless" and "torturous" feeling that clinical depression and unwanted fame brought her - CBS News
source.quoteSnippet: Her parents sleep in the living room so Billie has a bedroom and Finneas's old bedroom/studio is always available. There is no sound-proofing in their home recording studio.

nuggets.imageCaption: Billie Eilish, 17, reflecting on her early fame in her home.

nuggets.headline: Billie Eilish called 'bad guy' the 'stupidest song in the world'
nuggets.text: The signature bassline, kick drum, and amplified finger snaps of 'bad guy' are instantly recognizable, making it a defining pop-trap anthem. Lyrically, Billie said the song was about 'poking fun at everyone and their personas' – she suggests that real 'bad bitches' don't tell everyone they are. Years later, at 21, she humorously called 'bad guy' the 'stupidest song in the world,' but quickly added that it's 'really good' and intended to be 'goofy' and 'trolling.'

source.title: Billie Eilish says 'Bad Guy' is the "stupidest song in the world"
source.quoteSnippet: Billie Eilish, at 21 years old, called 'Bad Guy' the 'stupidest song in the world'

nuggets.imageCaption: Billie Eilish performing live, delivering her distinctive vocals.

nuggets.headline: Olivia Rodrigo shares Billie Eilish's knack for sharp, modern pop
nuggets.text: If this track hits right, you'll love Olivia Rodrigo. Like Billie, she crafts pop songs with a raw, candid edge and direct lyrics that feel incredibly personal. Her music often features a similar blend of confident, understated vocals and impactful, genre-bending production, making her another fantastic voice for modern youth. Check out her debut album, *SOUR*, for more of that sharp, intelligent pop.

source.title: Olivia Rodrigo
source.quoteSnippet: Olivia Rodrigo shares Billie Eilish's knack for sharp, modern pop.

nuggets.imageCaption: Olivia Rodrigo, a rising star known for her candid pop music.

artistSummary: Billie Eilish is a pop superstar from Los Angeles, born in 2001, who achieved global fame as a teenager. She co-writes and produces all her genre-defying music with her brother Finneas, candidly exploring themes like mental health. At just 18, she made history at the 2020 GRAMMYs, becoming the first woman to win all four major General Field Categories in one night.
